title: Guest Network Usage
dataSources: ISE guest portal logs, RADIUS accounting
app: `Splunk_TA_cisco-ise`, HPE Aruba ClearPass syslog, Forescout CounterACT syslog
spl:
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:guest"
| stats count, sum(session_duration_min) as total_min by sponsor, guest_type
| sort -count
description: Guest network monitoring ensures acceptable use and identifies capacity needs. Unusual patterns may indicate abuse.
value: Guest network monitoring ensures acceptable use and identifies capacity needs. Unusual patterns may indicate abuse.
implementation: Track guest portal registrations, sponsor activity, and session durations. Alert on excessive guest registrations from single sponsors. Monitor guest bandwidth usage. Report on guest network utilization.
visualization: Bar chart (guest registrations by sponsor), Line chart (guest sessions trend), Table (active guests).
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication
  by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src span=1h
| sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Anomalies from new SaaS app rollouts, browser updates, or vendor URL category changes.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_cisco-ise`, HPE Aruba ClearPass syslog, Forescout CounterACT syslog.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: ISE guest portal logs, RADIUS accounting.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Track guest portal registrations, sponsor activity, and session durations. Alert on excessive guest registrations from single sponsors. Monitor guest bandwidth usage. Report on guest network utilization.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:guest"
| stats count, sum(session_duration_min) as total_min by sponsor, guest_type
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Guest Network Usage** — Guest network monitoring ensures acceptable use and identifies capacity needs. Unusual patterns may indicate abuse.

Documented **Data sources**: ISE guest portal logs, RADIUS accounting. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_cisco-ise`, HPE Aruba ClearPass syslog, Forescout CounterACT syslog. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: nac; **sourcetype**: cisco:ise:guest. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=nac, sourcetype="cisco:ise:guest". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by sponsor, guest_type** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with Cisco ISE Live Log and endpoint details for the same sessions, MACs, and time range.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (guest registrations by sponsor), Line chart (guest sessions trend), Table (active guests).
grandmaExplanation: We use network security tools to watch who accesses what, help prevent data leaks, enforce network isolation, and secure cloud and remote access.
hardware: Cisco ISE 3515/3595/3615/3655/3695, ISE Virtual Appliance; HPE Aruba ClearPass C1000/C2000/C3000, ClearPass Virtual Appliance; Forescout CounterACT CT-xxxx, eyeExtend
